result:
Delivery complete. Story #9 **landed**.

## Summary

There was no Epic — this repo uses the Story-only v2 ticket model. The planning pipeline had created a single open Story, **#9** (`agent::ready`, `plan-run::f94f779a`), which I delivered via `/deliver 9 --yes`. As a single-Story run it executed the delivery engine inline.

**Terminal envelope:** `status: landed` — PR [#10](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-2c64f615/pull/10) MERGED (squash), checks `success`, all gates passed, Story #9 → `agent::done`.

### What shipped
- **Paginated `GET /reports/receivables`** — now takes `page`/`pageSize` via the shared `parsePagination()` and returns the standard `{ items, total, page, pageSize }` envelope, with `totalOutstandingCents` kept as the grand total across all pages. `total` = distinct issued-balance customer rows; `items` = the ordered page slice.
- **SQL stayed in the repository layer** — new `listReceivablesPage`, `countReceivables`, and `receivablesGrandTotalCents`; the service only assembles the envelope.
- **Default page size 20 → 25 in one place** (`DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE`), and reconciled the mirrored `limit = 20` default in `orders.repo.js#listOrders` so no endpoint keeps a 20 default.
- **New append-only migration `007-receivables-index.sql`** adding `idx_orders_status_customer (status, customer_id)`. `EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN` confirms the query uses it; the perf test (5,000 issued orders across 250 customers) responds in ~90ms, well under 2s.

### Verification
- Acceptance self-eval: **8/8 criteria met → `proceed`**.
- `npm test`: **157/157 pass** (including new AC-1…AC-6 report pagination/perf tests and updated pagination tests). `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint`: clean. Close-validation gates + inline code review passed (0 critical/high/medium; 1 advisory suggestion).

**Notes:** The close reported a degraded `baseFastForward` (local `main` fast-forward skipped — dirty tree from unrelated stray files belonging to another session) and a disjoint wrong-tree telemetry comment for those same stray files. Neither affects the land — the merge stands. Being a single-Story run, no per-run epilogue applies.
